batsmen.name: Virat Kohli
batsmen.description: Cover drive king. Flick through mid-wicket. Pulls anything short. Tracks the ball late, plays orthodox spin with ease — but the late wobble of drift and a sharp googly still get him every now and then.

batsmen.name: Shubman Gill
batsmen.description: Current India ODI captain, technically the most-correct young bat in world cricket. Straight drives down the ground are his signature. Late cuts the moving ball, plays patient defensive — but the moving ball outside off has caught him out at the highest level.

batsmen.name: Rohit Sharma
batsmen.description: Lofted straight drives. Pulls off the front foot from good length. Stronger on the leg side; vulnerable to the off-stump fishing line. Reads pace easily; struggles against sharp drift and the carrom ball.

batsmen.name: Suryakumar Yadav
batsmen.description: World #1 T20I batter. 360-degree hitter — scoops, ramps, reverse sweeps. Eats spin alive in the middle overs. Sometimes leaves a gap going across; vulnerable to a sharp seamer angling back in.

batsmen.name: Yashasvi Jaiswal
batsmen.description: LH explosive opener. Multiple T20I 100s, IPL Orange Cap contender. Big sixes over long-on, drives over extra cover. Vulnerable to good-length pace squaring him up.

batsmen.name: Tilak Varma
batsmen.description: Mumbai Indians' aggressive left-hander. Strong on the leg side, plays the pull and slog. Solid against drift and top-spin; vulnerable to early-innings new-ball pace and the short-ball outside off.

batsmen.name: Rinku Singh
batsmen.description: KKR death-overs finisher. Famous for clearing the rope on the leg side — the "5 sixes in an over" guy. Limited new-ball game; vulnerable in early-innings phases. Reads slower balls and cutters from death-overs experience.

batsmen.name: Abhishek Sharma
batsmen.description: Sunrisers Hyderabad explosive opener. Hits big in powerplay — drives over extra cover, pulls anything short. Plays the new ball well; vulnerable when forced to defend on the back foot, especially against good-length balls in the corridor.

batsmen.name: Riyan Parag
batsmen.description: Rajasthan Royals 2024 IPL breakout (528 runs). Aggressive middle-order, can clear the rope occasionally but lacks consistent boundary-hitting reliability. No real T20I career.

batsmen.name: Sai Sudharsan
batsmen.description: Tamil Nadu left-handed opener. 527 IPL runs in 2024 for GT. Technically correct, picks length well — but lacks the gear-shift to clear ropes consistently. T20I debut in 2024.

batsmen.name: Rahul Tewatia
batsmen.description: RR/GT lower-order finisher. Famous 5 sixes vs Cottrell in IPL 2020. Slogs to leg, but inconsistent — hits or misses with little in between. No real T20I career.

batsmen.name: Deepak Hooda
batsmen.description: Off-spin all-rounder. T20Is sporadically — doesn't quite make the senior squad consistently. Hits through the line but technically limited; struggles outside off.

batsmen.name: Venkatesh Iyer
batsmen.description: KKR opener, tall left-hander. Big-hitting on his day but inconsistent. Played a few T20Is in 2021-22, has fallen out of the picture since.

batsmen.name: Travis Head
batsmen.description: Aggressive left-handed opener. Smashes through the off side off the front foot, pulls anything short, brutal vs spin in the middle overs. T20 WC 2024 best XI; held up under extreme pressure (137 in the 2023 ODI WC final). Vulnerable mainly to early-innings new-ball pace.

batsmen.name: Mitchell Marsh
batsmen.description: Current AUS T20I captain. **Player of the Match in the 2021 T20 WC Final** (77\* off 50 — the trophy-winning innings). Lofted drives, pulls anything short, all-rounder pedigree. (All-rounder split — keeps his existing Bronze bowler card.)

batsmen.name: Glenn Maxwell
batsmen.description: 360-degree hitter. Reverse sweeps, switch hits, scoops over the keeper. 2023 ODI WC double-century. Genius-or-out: brilliant when set, vulnerable when starting. Eats most spin alive but the carrom ball has caught him before.

batsmen.name: Marcus Stoinis
batsmen.description: Power hitter in the death. Lofts straight, drives through the off, pulls anything short. T20 WC 2024 best XI. Handles all pace variations with strength but can be tricked by quality spin.

batsmen.name: Steve Smith
batsmen.description: Test/ODI legend, current AUS T20I returnee. Quirky technique — leaves leg stump exposed, then flicks across with wristy precision. Anchor with sudden gear shifts; world-class against pace and spin alike.

batsmen.name: Tim David
batsmen.description: Singapore-born T20 mercenary. MI/RCB. Massive death-overs hitter, leg-side dominant — the slog and the hook are his go-tos. Reads slower balls and cutters from his death-overs experience.

batsmen.name: Josh Inglis
batsmen.description: Wicketkeeper-bat. Clean striker through the off side. T20I appearances steady but not first-choice. Plays spin from the BBL with comfort; struggles against raw pace and short balls into the body.

batsmen.name: Cameron Green
batsmen.description: Tall all-rounder. IPL with RCB/MI, has played T20Is for Australia. Strong technique handles the new ball well; vulnerable to short-pitched bowling and the fishing line.

batsmen.name: Mitchell Owen
batsmen.description: Hobart Hurricanes opener. BBL hitter, very limited international career. Goes hard from ball one but raw technique.

batsmen.name: Cooper Connolly
batsmen.description: Young left-hander, fringe of Australian selection. All-rounder, tidy with the bat but limited power at international level so far.

batsmen.name: Jake Fraser-McGurk
batsmen.description: Rajasthan Royals' explosive opener. IPL 2024 breakout — second-fastest IPL fifty ever. Has played T20Is for Australia but isn't established. Aggressive but raw — hits big when timing it, vulnerable when forced to defend.

batsmen.name: Matthew Renshaw
batsmen.description: Left-handed grinder. Domestic Sheffield Shield performer with limited T20I exposure. Defensive mindset — plays the percentages, vulnerable when asked to score quickly.

batsmen.name: Ben McDermott
batsmen.description: Hobart Hurricanes BBL middle-order hitter. 8 T20I caps for Australia (last 2022). Specialist batter — back-foot pull and leg-side power. Vulnerable in the corridor outside off.

batsmen.name: Jos Buttler
batsmen.description: England's T20 captain and talisman. Destructive keeper-bat — ramps, scoops, lofted hits over extra cover. T20 WC 2022 winner. Plays spin from the BBL/IPL with comfort. Vulnerable mainly to skiddy seam back into him at the stumps.

batsmen.name: Joe Root
batsmen.description: England Test/ODI run machine, T20I returnee. Sweep specialist vs spin in the middle overs, late dabs anywhere on the off side, classical strengths in the corridor. Vulnerable mainly to high pace banged in short.

batsmen.name: Harry Brook
batsmen.description: England's #3, T20 WC star. Drives beautifully over extra cover, pulls anything short, all-round attacking. Technically sound for an aggressive batter — eats spin alive but vulnerable in the corridor outside off.

batsmen.name: Phil Salt
batsmen.description: Explosive powerplay opener. Multiple T20I centuries in 2024. Pulls anything short for huge runs, drives over the off side, scoops occasionally. Pace specialist; struggles when the ball turns.

batsmen.name: Alex Hales
batsmen.description: RH explosive opener, T20 WC 2022 squad member. IPL star (RCB), recent T20I returnee. Drives, pulls, lofts — power-hitter from ball one. Vulnerable to the swinging new ball.

batsmen.name: Liam Livingstone
batsmen.description: Power-hitter who switch-hits and reverse-sweeps. T20 WC 2024 squad. Smashes most spin and reads slower balls. Vulnerable to good lengths and the moving ball outside off.

batsmen.name: Ben Duckett
batsmen.description: Left-handed opener. Sweeps and reverse-sweeps everything — a spin specialist. T20 WC 2024 squad. Vulnerable to pace and short balls.

batsmen.name: Sam Curran
batsmen.description: Left-arm all-rounder (batter card; bowler card carries his death-overs role). T20 WC 2022 Player of the Tournament for his bowling — useful aggressive lower-order hitter when needed.

batsmen.name: Jason Roy
batsmen.description: Former explosive opener. Declining form, inconsistent at international level now.

batsmen.name: Sam Billings
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. KKR IPL veteran, ~8 T20I caps for England (last 2022). Counter-attacking middle-order role: ramps and scoops vs pace, sweeps vs spin. Limited international footprint keeps him at Bronze.

batsmen.name: Tom Banton
batsmen.description: Flashy keeper-bat. Reverse scoops. High risk, high reward.

batsmen.name: Dan Lawrence
batsmen.description: County talent. Limited T20I exposure. Technically decent but untested.

outcome.mode: flicks in the air to mid-wicket

batsmen.name: Mason Crane
batsmen.description: Leg-spin bowler, genuine tailender with the bat. 1 Test, minimal T20I.

batsmen.name: Heinrich Klaasen
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. SA's most feared T20 hitter. IPL MVP-tier; T20 WC 2024 best SA bat (190 runs, SR 137). Destroys spin and pace alike — only new-ball swing troubles him.

batsmen.name: AB de Villiers
batsmen.description: Mr 360 — retired internationals 2018, 78 T20Is, IPL legend (RCB). Plays every shot in the book and several that aren't. Reverse sweeps over slip, scoops over fine leg, ramps the quick. The most complete T20 batter of his generation.

batsmen.name: Quinton de Kock
batsmen.description: Left-handed keeper-bat. Retired internationally in 2024 but still IPL/SA20 star. T20 WC 2024 leading SA scorer (232 runs, SR 140). Pull, drive, sweep — pure pace specialist.

batsmen.name: David Miller
batsmen.description: Left-handed death-overs finisher. IPL legend, T20 WC 2024 finalist. Lofts straight, flicks square. Cutters and slower balls trouble him.

batsmen.name: Faf du Plessis
batsmen.description: Retired internationals 2021, 50 T20Is, IPL captain (CSK / RCB). Anchor with strong technique and clever pace control. Drives and pulls with classical timing.

batsmen.name: Aiden Markram
batsmen.description: SA T20I captain. Right-handed top-order, technically correct. Beautiful drives; reads spin well via own off-spin. Vulnerable on the pads.

batsmen.name: Tristan Stubbs
batsmen.description: Hard-hitting middle-order RHB. Top-5 in T20I rankings recently. Power over finesse; vulnerable around off stump.

batsmen.name: Ryan Rickelton
batsmen.description: Young left-handed keeper-bat. SA20 standout, breaking into the international XI. Sweeps and reverse-sweeps spin; vulnerable to short balls and the moving ball outside off.

batsmen.name: Reeza Hendricks
batsmen.description: Right-handed accumulator opener. Solid against the new ball; lacks T20 acceleration.

batsmen.name: Rassie van der Dussen
batsmen.description: Technically correct middle-order RHB. Anchors but lacks T20 ceiling.

batsmen.name: Donovan Ferreira
batsmen.description: Young IPL/SA20 power hitter. Raw at international level — clears the rope when he times it, gets out trying when he doesn't.

batsmen.name: Wiaan Mulder
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; medium-pace bowler split below). Not a T20 specialist with the bat — useful lower-order overs.

batsmen.name: Rilee Rossouw
batsmen.description: Left-handed power hitter. 36 T20Is for SA in his 2014–2016 prime + 2022 WC century cameo. Aggressive Bronze profile — clears the rope or holes out.

batsmen.name: Kane Williamson
batsmen.description: NZ T20I icon. Test-class technique applied to T20 — the anchor who guides chases. T20 WC 2024 leading NZ scorer (139 runs). Reads spin and pace equally; sweep, drive, dab — every shot in the textbook.

batsmen.name: Brendon McCullum
batsmen.description: Legend — retired 2016, RH explosive opener, 71 T20Is at SR ~136. Inaugural KKR captain (105 in IPL's first-ever match). Slogs everything in sight, pulls anything short, ramps the quick. Mid-2010s game-changing T20 mindset.

batsmen.name: Glenn Phillips
batsmen.description: NZ's most explosive current T20 hitter. Big sixes over midwicket, switch hits, ramps. Has a Bronze part-time off-spin bowler card. Slower balls and cutters get him.

batsmen.name: Devon Conway
batsmen.description: Left-handed opener. Technically superb. Drives off both feet, sweeps spin. Vulnerable to short pace at the body.

batsmen.name: Ross Taylor
batsmen.description: Retired 2022, classy RH middle-order, 102 T20Is. Solid technique with good power. NZ stalwart across two decades, a long-time anchor in the middle order.

batsmen.name: Mark Chapman
batsmen.description: Left-handed stroke-maker. Growing international presence after a strong 2024. Cover drives and sweeps; struggles with the moving ball outside off.

batsmen.name: Daryl Mitchell
batsmen.description: Gutsy right-handed middle-order. Works the ball smartly rather than hitting through it. T20 WC 2024 staple.

batsmen.name: Rachin Ravindra
batsmen.description: Left-handed all-rounder (batter card; LA orthodox bowler split below). Elegant top-order bat, picks length early.

batsmen.name: Colin Munro
batsmen.description: Left-handed explosive opener. 65 T20Is for NZ in his 2014–2020 prime, three T20I centuries. Aggressive Bronze profile — slogs over midwicket, ramps; gets out trying to clear the rope.

batsmen.name: Jimmy Neesham
batsmen.description: Left-handed all-rounder finisher. Big hitter at death; loose technically.

batsmen.name: Will Young
batsmen.description: Right-handed Test specialist batting in T20s. Technically clean, lacks T20 power.

batsmen.name: Tom Blundell
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. Gritty but not a T20 natural.

batsmen.name: Michael Bracewell
batsmen.description: Off-spinning all-rounder (batter card; Bronze bowler split below). Right-handed, sweeps and reverse-sweeps.

batsmen.name: Babar Azam
batsmen.description: Right-handed PAK icon. Multi-time world #1 ranked T20I batter. Cover drive maestro, anchor archetype, scores all around the wicket. Plays pace and spin with equal class.

batsmen.name: Shoaib Malik
batsmen.description: Retired 2024 — **124 T20Is, the most appearances ever for any player**. T20 WC 2009 winner, all-format PAK stalwart across two decades. Sweeps and reverse-laps anything from spinners; lofts straight off pace.

batsmen.name: Mohammad Rizwan
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. World #1 T20I ranked at multiple points. Accumulator with class — drives, flicks, anchors. Vulnerable to short pace at the body.

batsmen.name: Fakhar Zaman
batsmen.description: Aggressive left-handed opener. CT 2017 final centurion. Goes hard early, drives + sweeps. Vulnerable to the moving ball into the stumps.

batsmen.name: Mohammad Hafeez
batsmen.description: Retired 2024, 119 T20Is, RH all-rounder, "The Professor". Cleanly times the cover drive and pull, sweeps spin from the BBL/PSL/IPL experience. PAK middle-order anchor across the 2010s.

batsmen.name: Salman Agha
batsmen.description: Current PAK T20I captain. Right-handed middle-order, off-spinning all-rounder. Smart cricketer rather than pure power.

batsmen.name: Saim Ayub
batsmen.description: Exciting young left-handed opener. Clean ball striker, big sixes off the front foot. Vulnerable to the ball nipping back into the stumps.

batsmen.name: Shahid Afridi
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s legend. 99 T20Is for PAK (last 2018), captain across formats, T20 WC 2007/2009 hero. Hit-or-miss slogger by his late career — averaged ~17 in T20Is at SR 150. Pure boundary-or-out.

batsmen.name: Iftikhar Ahmed
batsmen.description: Right-handed veteran middle-order finisher. Power over finesse; falls to spin (also Bronze part-time off-spin bowler split below).

batsmen.name: Usman Khan
batsmen.description: Young aggressive right-handed batter. Raw talent, growing international exposure.

batsmen.name: Asif Ali
batsmen.description: Right-handed death-overs power hitter. 50+ T20Is for PAK, T20 WC 2021 cameos. Hits boundaries at the back end of the innings — or holes out trying.

batsmen.name: Faheem Ashraf
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; medium-fast bowler split below). Lower-order pinch hitter, useful overs.

batsmen.name: Shadab Khan
batsmen.description: Right-handed leg-spin all-rounder (batter card; Bronze bowler split below). Clean lower-order hitter — was Silver-tier at his peak; current form has dropped him.

batsmen.name: Pathum Nissanka
batsmen.description: Right-handed opener. SL's most complete current T20 bat — only SL T20I centurion (vs Bangladesh 2024). Cover drives, cuts, flicks; reads spin and pace equally.

batsmen.name: Kumar Sangakkara
batsmen.description: Legend — retired internationals 2015, 56 T20Is, T20 WC 2014 winner, all-time-great LH keeper-bat. Glides, sweeps, drives off both feet, reverse-laps spin. The most technically-correct attacking bat of his era.

batsmen.name: Charith Asalanka
batsmen.description: Current SL T20I captain. Left-handed stroke-maker, elegant drives and sweeps.

batsmen.name: Kusal Mendis
batsmen.description: Aggressive right-handed keeper-bat. Pulls and cuts with power. Recent IPL form.

batsmen.name: Mahela Jayawardene
batsmen.description: Legend — retired 2014, 55 T20Is, T20 WC 2014 Player of the Tournament. RH right-hand stylist with the wristy whip through midwicket and the late cut signature. Captain across formats.

batsmen.name: Kamindu Mendis
batsmen.description: Left-handed rising star. Sweeps and reverse-sweeps; unique ambidextrous part-time spinner (split below).

batsmen.name: Kusal Perera
batsmen.description: Explosive left-handed opener. Goes hard from ball one. 60+ T20Is, still in T20I mix.

batsmen.name: Tillakaratne Dilshan
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s legend. 79 T20Is, 2014 T20 WC winner, "Dilscoop" inventor. Right-handed opener with the full shot range — late-career SR ~120 puts him at Silver, not Gold.

batsmen.name: Bhanuka Rajapaksa
batsmen.description: Left-handed power hitter. T20 WC 2022 final hero (71*), recognizable middle-order finisher.

batsmen.name: Avishka Fernando
batsmen.description: Right-handed opener. Recognizable from his 2019 ODI WC century. Solid technique, limited T20 acceleration.

batsmen.name: Niroshan Dickwella
batsmen.description: Left-handed keeper-bat. 65+ T20Is, mid-2010s prime + recent. Cheeky scoops and sweeps; plays across the line and falls LBW.

batsmen.name: Dunith Wellalage
batsmen.description: Young left-arm spin all-rounder (batter card; Silver LA orthodox bowler split below). Useful lower-order bat.

outcome.mode: mistimed pull, caught at deep midwicket

batsmen.name: Dasun Shanaka
batsmen.description: Right-handed former T20I captain (all-rounder split — Bronze bowler below). Death-overs slogger; explosive when timed, holes out when not.

batsmen.name: Nicholas Pooran
batsmen.description: Left-handed destroyer. IPL/T20 league superstar, current WI T20I vice-captain. Hits sixes across all lines; reads spin and pace equally — only the moving ball outside off troubles him.

batsmen.name: Chris Gayle
batsmen.description: All-time T20 batting legend — retired 2021, 79 T20Is + ~450 T20s globally. Universe Boss. Stands still, walks across, hits over long-on for fun. The original T20 superhero. Stroke-range universal, only the moving new ball troubles him.

batsmen.name: Andre Russell
batsmen.description: Right-handed global T20 icon (batter card; Silver bowler split below). Power hitter at all angles; even in his late career still clears the rope. Slower balls are his only real trouble.

batsmen.name: Brandon King
batsmen.description: Current WI T20I opener. Right-handed top-order power — drives through the line, pulls anything short. Vulnerable to the ball nipping back into the stumps.

batsmen.name: Kieron Pollard
batsmen.description: Retired internationals 2022, 101 T20Is, MI IPL captain legend. Massive death-overs power-hitter — slogs over midwicket for sport, lofts straight, fearless against any pace.

batsmen.name: Shimron Hetmyer
batsmen.description: Left-handed death-overs finisher. Leg-side power; goes hard from ball one when set.

batsmen.name: Rovman Powell
batsmen.description: Former WI T20I captain. Right-handed big-hitter, T20 WC 2024 staple. Inconsistent but devastating on song.

batsmen.name: Sherfane Rutherford
batsmen.description: Left-handed finisher. IPL 2024 form, massive leg-side hitter.

batsmen.name: Johnson Charles
batsmen.description: Right-handed explosive opener. Smashes early, gets out early.

batsmen.name: Roston Chase
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Bronze off-spin bowler split below). Steady accumulator, lacks T20 acceleration.

batsmen.name: Shai Hope
batsmen.description: Right-handed WI ODI captain. Technically clean — more ODI than T20 specialist, hence Bronze. Cover drives and flicks; struggles with short balls at the body.

batsmen.name: Marlon Samuels
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s legend. T20 WC 2012 (78) AND 2016 (85*) final hero — only player to score 50+ in two T20 WC finals. Right-handed middle-order, retired 2020. Languid technique that frustrated bowlers and slogged on cue.

batsmen.name: Romario Shepherd
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Bronze medium-fast bowler split below). Power at death, raw technique.

batsmen.name: Litton Das
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. Brief world #1 T20I ranking moment in 2022; remains BAN's most explosive top-order option. Pulls, drives, sweeps; reads spin and pace equally — only the moving ball outside off troubles him.

batsmen.name: Junaid Siddique
batsmen.description: Early-2010s LH opener — 11 T20Is + 50+ ODIs, BAN's first-generation T20 batsman. Ground stroke-maker with a strong off-side game. Played at the dawn of T20Is when Bangladesh were finding their feet at the format.

batsmen.name: Shakib Al Hasan
batsmen.description: Left-handed all-time great all-rounder (batter card; Gold bowler split below). Sweeps and drives, plays spin as well as anyone in the modern game. Retired from internationals 2024; still active in T20 leagues.

batsmen.name: Towhid Hridoy
batsmen.description: Right-handed rising middle-order. SR 130+ across recent T20Is. Clean striker, improving fast — pulls and lofts; vulnerable around off stump.

batsmen.name: Imrul Kayes
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s LH opener, 19 T20Is + 78 ODIs. Solid technique, anchors the top order, drives + flicks. Part of BAN's transitional generation between the early days and the current era.

batsmen.name: Najmul Hossain Shanto
batsmen.description: Current BAN T20I captain. Left-handed top-order, elegant but inconsistent at the international level. Drives and sweeps; struggles around off stump.

batsmen.name: Tamim Iqbal
batsmen.description: Left-handed legend. BAN's all-time T20I top scorer, retired internationally 2023. Anchor opener with classic technique — drives and flicks; vulnerable to short pace and the ball into the pads.

batsmen.name: Soumya Sarkar
batsmen.description: Left-handed opener (batter card; Bronze bowler split below). Goes hard with the new ball — explosive when set, falls early when not.

batsmen.name: Mushfiqur Rahim
batsmen.description: Right-handed veteran keeper-bat. Anchor type, lacking T20 power now.

batsmen.name: Mahmudullah Riyad
batsmen.description: Right-handed veteran finisher. T20 WC 2024 squad. Cool head at the death — slogs over midwicket, lofts straight; struggles vs the moving ball outside off.

batsmen.name: Mehidy Hasan Miraz
batsmen.description: Right-handed off-spinning all-rounder (batter card; Silver bowler split below). Lower-order bat — sweeps and works.

batsmen.name: Afif Hossain
batsmen.description: Left-handed all-rounder. Current form has dropped him from Silver — sweeps and finishes when set; falls early when not.

batsmen.name: Rishad Hossain
batsmen.description: Right-handed leg-spinning all-rounder (batter card; Silver bowler split below). Tail-ender bat — slogs when he can.

batsmen.name: Sikandar Raza
batsmen.description: Right-handed ZIM GOAT, current T20I captain. IPL/PSL star, T20 WC 2024 standout. Plays spin and pace beautifully — only the moving ball outside off troubles him (also Gold off-spin bowler split below).

batsmen.name: Andy Flower
batsmen.description: Honorary Elite — ZIM's all-time greatest batter (LH keeper-bat), retired 2003 just before T20Is existed. Wisden Cricketer of the Year 2002. World #1 ranked Test batter for stretches. Career-tier elite even without T20I caps. Sweep + flick + classical cuts.

batsmen.name: Craig Ervine
batsmen.description: Left-handed veteran top-order. Solid technique, experienced campaigner. Drives + sweeps.

batsmen.name: Sean Williams
batsmen.description: Left-handed veteran all-rounder (batter card; Silver LA orthodox bowler split below). Sweeps and drives, anchors the middle order.

batsmen.name: Hamilton Masakadza
batsmen.description: Legend — retired 2019, 66 T20Is, captain. ZIM's most-capped T20I bat at the time of retirement. Anchor opener, classy drives, plays both pace and spin patiently.

batsmen.name: Regis Chakabva
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. Aggressive opener, quick scorer.

batsmen.name: Wessly Madhevere
batsmen.description: Right-handed young top-order (batter card; Bronze bowler split below). Stylish, growing T20 game.

batsmen.name: Ryan Burl
batsmen.description: Left-handed leg-spinning all-rounder (batter card; Silver bowler split below). Power hitter at death.

batsmen.name: Tadiwanashe Marumani
batsmen.description: Right-handed young opener, emerging at international level.

batsmen.name: Brendan Taylor
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s legend. ZIM's all-time leading run-scorer across formats, 50+ T20Is. Right-handed keeper-bat, retired internationally 2021. Late-career drives and flicks; vulnerable to short pace.

batsmen.name: Joylord Gumbie
batsmen.description: Right-handed middle-order, current ZIM squad fringe.

batsmen.name: Milton Shumba
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Bronze medium-pace bowler split below). Lower-order utility.

batsmen.name: Clive Madande
batsmen.description: Right-handed young keeper-bat. Domestic-level.

batsmen.name: Rahmanullah Gurbaz
batsmen.description: Right-handed explosive opener. IPL star, T20 WC 2024 top AFG scorer (281 runs). Hits sixes everywhere; reads spin and pace equally — only the moving ball outside off troubles him.

batsmen.name: Asghar Afghan
batsmen.description: Former AFG captain, retired 2021, 66 T20Is — most-experienced AFG bat of the rise-of-Afghanistan era. Solid middle-order anchor who carried the team through their early international growth.

batsmen.name: Ibrahim Zadran
batsmen.description: Current AFG ODI captain. Right-handed classy anchor — drives, flicks, works the ball. Growing T20 game.

batsmen.name: Hazratullah Zazai
batsmen.description: Left-handed explosive opener. Owns one of the highest individual T20I scores (162* vs Ireland 2019). Goes hard from ball one with raw power.

batsmen.name: Mohammad Nabi
batsmen.description: Right-handed veteran all-rounder (batter card; Bronze off-spin bowler split below). Smart middle-order, sweeps and works.

batsmen.name: Najibullah Zadran
batsmen.description: Left-handed leg-side power hitter. T20I regular.

batsmen.name: Azmatullah Omarzai
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Silver bowler split below). T20 WC 2024 staple (158 runs SR 132). Clean ball striker.

batsmen.name: Sediqullah Atal
batsmen.description: Young right-handed opener, recent T20I caps. Aggressive but raw.

batsmen.name: Mohammad Shahzad
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s legend. Flamboyant right-handed keeper-bat, 80+ T20Is for AFG, retired ~2020. Slog-or-out Bronze archetype — clears the rope or holes out.

batsmen.name: Gulbadin Naib
batsmen.description: Right-handed veteran all-rounder (batter card; Bronze medium-pace bowler split below). Past his prime but still in T20I plans.

batsmen.name: Karim Janat
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Bronze medium-fast bowler split below). Current T20I middle-order.

batsmen.name: Darwish Rasooli
batsmen.description: Young right-handed middle-order, developing talent.

batsmen.name: Paul Stirling
batsmen.description: Right-handed Ireland T20 GOAT. 100+ T20Is, 10+ T20I 50s. Explosive opener — smashes through off side, lofts over midwicket, sweeps spin. Reads pace and spin equally; only the moving ball outside off troubles him.

batsmen.name: Eoin Morgan
batsmen.description: Multi-nation legend — played 23 ODIs for Ireland 2006-2009 before switching to England, where he became the country's most successful T20I captain (T20 WC 2022 winner). LH explosive middle-order, sweeps, lofts, switch-hits. Anchored England's white-ball revolution.

batsmen.name: Andrew Balbirnie
batsmen.description: Right-handed elegant anchor, former Ireland captain. Beautiful drives off both feet. Vulnerable to short pace at the body.

batsmen.name: Harry Tector
batsmen.description: Right-handed young middle-order (batter card; Bronze part-time off-spin bowler split below). Technically sound, growing power game.

batsmen.name: Ed Joyce
batsmen.description: Multi-nation career — IRE → ENG → IRE. LH classy middle-order, retired 2018. Played in two ODI WCs across both countries. Drives off both feet, sweeps spin from his county-cricket pedigree.

batsmen.name: Lorcan Tucker
batsmen.description: Right-handed keeper-bat. Aggressive, quick scorer. Pulls and lofts; struggles with the moving ball outside off.

batsmen.name: Curtis Campher
batsmen.description: South African-born right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Gold bowler split below). Useful middle-order, solid technique.

outcome.mode: mistimed pull, caught at deep midwicket

batsmen.name: George Dockrell
batsmen.description: Left-handed all-rounder (batter card; Silver LA orthodox bowler split below). Lower middle-order sweeper.

batsmen.name: Kevin O'Brien
batsmen.description: Mid-2010s legend. Iconic 2011 ODI WC century vs England (then-fastest WC century, off 50 balls). Right-handed, 100+ T20Is, retired 2021. Slog-or-out late-career profile.

batsmen.name: Gareth Delany
batsmen.description: Right-handed off-spin all-rounder (batter card; Silver bowler split below). Handy lower-order bat.

batsmen.name: Ross Adair
batsmen.description: Right-handed young top-order. Limited T20I exposure, domestic talent.

outcome.mode: mistimed pull, caught at deep midwicket

batsmen.name: Neil Rock
batsmen.description: Left-handed young keeper-bat. Domestic-level.

batsmen.name: Graham Hume
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder (batter card; Bronze medium-fast bowler split below). Tail-ender quality with the bat.

batsmen.name: Paras Khadka
batsmen.description: Former NEP captain, RH all-rounder. Nepal cricket's pioneer figure who led the team to T20I status. Retired ~2021. Drives and pulls with veteran composure.

batsmen.name: Aasif Sheikh
batsmen.description: Current RH keeper-bat. Solid technique, current Nepal mainstay at the top of the order.

batsmen.name: Babar Hayat
batsmen.description: Right-handed top-order, longtime HK stalwart. 60+ T20Is, scored 122* in a T20 WC qualifier vs PNG. Drives and pulls; vulnerable to short pace.

batsmen.name: Rahul Dravid
batsmen.description: Multi-nation cricket trivia gold — played a single Pro40 game for Scotland in 2003 as a marquee signing. Mostly known as India's 'The Wall' — the ultimate Test/ODI anchor. Classical technique, drives, late cuts, defends.

batsmen.name: Kyle Coetzer
batsmen.description: Long-time Scotland captain, retired ~2022, 70+ T20Is across formats. RH classy opener, anchor archetype, took SCO through multiple T20 WCs.

batsmen.name: Richie Berrington
batsmen.description: Right-handed Scotland T20I captain. 110+ T20Is, T20 WC stalwart (60 vs WI 2021). Anchor with the full shot range.

batsmen.name: George Munsey
batsmen.description: Current LH explosive opener, 50+ T20Is, T20 WC squads. Big sixes from ball one. Vulnerable to good-length pace.

batsmen.name: Corey Anderson
batsmen.description: Multi-nation career — NZ→USA. Played for NZ until 2018 (LH all-rounder, **once-fastest ODI century at 36 balls** vs WI 2014). Switched to USA 2023, T20 WC 2024 squad. Power-hitter top-order.

batsmen.name: Aaron Jones
batsmen.description: Left-handed middle-order. T20 WC 2024 hero — 94* vs Canada in the opener; USA reached the Super Eights. Big sixes over the leg side.

batsmen.name: Monank Patel
batsmen.description: Current USA T20I captain, RH keeper-bat. T20 WC 2024 led USA to Super Eights. Smart accumulator + power finisher.

batsmen.name: Ryan ten Doeschate
batsmen.description: NED's most-iconic all-rounder — 33 T20Is for NED + IPL (KKR) / CPL legend. Career T20 SR ~135 across all leagues. The associate-cricket Elite. RH power-hitting middle-order with full shot range.

batsmen.name: Bas de Leede
batsmen.description: Right-handed all-rounder. T20 WC 2024 squad. Knocked SA out of T20 WC 2022 (his father Tim played for NED too).

outcome.mode: mistimed pull, caught at deep midwicket

batsmen.name: Gerhard Erasmus
batsmen.description: Right-handed Namibia captain. NAM's #1 T20 batter, captained the team that beat Sri Lanka at T20 WC 2021.

bowlers.name: Jasprit Bumrah
bowlers.description: Signature: full middle stump yorker. Unorthodox action, late seam movement off the deck, world-class slower-ball deception (knuckle ball, slower bouncer). Best T20 death bowler alive.

bowlers.name: Yuzvendra Chahal
bowlers.description: Most T20I wickets in India's history (96 in 80 matches). Right-arm leg-spinner — lethal disguised googly + skiddy quicker arm-ball. RR/RCB IPL stalwart and India's middle-overs match-winner across the late 2010s and 2020s. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Kuldeep Yadav
bowlers.description: Left-arm wrist-spinner. Stock ball is the leg-spinner; the chinaman googly is hard to read and is his signature wicket-taking variation. Middle-overs wicket-taker.

bowlers.name: Arshdeep Singh
bowlers.description: Left-arm pacer. Full Outside off line, in-swing back into the right-hander from the natural left-arm angle. Powerplay swing specialist who also bowls death overs.

bowlers.name: Mohammed Siraj
bowlers.description: Right-arm first-choice quick. Hits the deck hard with seam off the surface. The 6-wicket Asia Cup 2023 final spell vs Sri Lanka cemented his role as India's powerplay enforcer.

bowlers.name: Avesh Khan
bowlers.description: Reliable IPL good-length seamer. Couple of T20Is but mostly an LSG/RR/PBKS workhorse. Hits hard lengths consistently — no signature variation, just discipline.

bowlers.name: Hardik Pandya
bowlers.description: Medium-fast all-rounder. Heavy back-of-length hit-the-deck pacer. India's regular sixth-bowler when fit. No signature variation in T20s any more — just hard length and aggression.

bowlers.name: Mayank Yadav
bowlers.description: Lucknow Super Giants. IPL 2024 breakout — consistent 150+ kph express pace. Bouncer specialist. Injury-hampered, so no real international career to speak of.

bowlers.name: Harshal Patel
bowlers.description: IPL death-overs specialist. Famous for cutters, but Bronze bowlers carry no specific variation — he's just a back-of-length pacer here, hoping the batter mistimes.

bowlers.name: Mayank Markande
bowlers.description: Leg-spinner, 1 T20I cap (2018). IPL with SRH/MI/RR. Stock leg-break attacking the leg-stump line.

bowlers.name: Umran Malik
bowlers.description: Raw pace, 150+ kph. Short of length specialist. Thrilling but erratic — limited T20Is, IPL-only most of his career.

bowlers.name: Ravi Bishnoi
bowlers.description: Leg-break bowler with quicker pace through the air. Has played T20Is but currently fringe. Attacking line at the stumps.

bowlers.name: Mukesh Kumar
bowlers.description: Delhi Capitals seamer. Limited T20I exposure. Reliable IPL line-and-length bowler — no special variation, just plays the percentages.

bowlers.name: Mitchell Starc
bowlers.description: Left-arm express. Late in-swing into right-handers from the natural left-arm angle, devastating death-overs yorkers, world-class slower-ball variations (slower bouncers and slower yorkers). Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule — only one fires per ball, no stacking.

bowlers.name: Pat Cummins
bowlers.description: AUS Test captain + world-class fast bowler across all formats. Hits the deck hard with bounce, late seam off the surface, signature slower ball at the death. T20I selections sporadic but career-tier elite. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Adam Zampa
bowlers.description: Australia's first-choice leg-spinner. T20 WC 2024 best XI. Stock leg-break with a disguised googly that's caught out plenty of right-handers. Gets drift and turn on any surface.

bowlers.name: Josh Hazlewood
bowlers.description: Metronomic seamer. Good length Outside off relentlessly — the corridor of uncertainty. Tier-based wide-call risk on Outside off (Gold = 15%) is the trade-off for the fishing line. Miserly economy.

bowlers.name: Sean Abbott
bowlers.description: Current AUS T20I death-overs specialist. BBL veteran with cutter + slower-ball variations as his trade. Reliable in pressure overs.

bowlers.name: Nathan Ellis
bowlers.description: Death-overs specialist. Slower balls and cutters in real cricket — Silver bowlers carry no skill in v1.1, so he's just a wily yorker-length pacer here.

bowlers.name: Marcus Stoinis
bowlers.description: Medium-pace all-rounder. Hit-the-deck good-length seamer in real cricket — Silver bowlers carry no specific skill in v1.1.

bowlers.name: Xavier Bartlett
bowlers.description: Emerging Brisbane Heat quick. Tall, hits good length with bounce. Limited Australian career so far. No specific skill at the Silver tier.

bowlers.name: Ben Dwarshuis
bowlers.description: Left-arm medium-pacer. BBL veteran with very limited international exposure. Bowls into the body from the left-arm angle — no specific skill at the Bronze tier.

bowlers.name: Matt Kuhnemann
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox spinner. Test-class but a tail-ender in T20s. Economical line-and-length, rarely a wicket-taking threat at this format.

bowlers.name: Cooper Connolly
bowlers.description: Part-time left-arm spin. Filler overs when the front-line bowlers are spent. Steady but unthreatening.

bowlers.name: Cameron Green
bowlers.description: Medium-fast all-rounder (bowler card, paired with his Silver batter card via the all-rounder split). Hit-the-deck good-length seamer at this format — useful 5th-bowler with no specific skill at the Bronze tier.

bowlers.name: Mitchell Marsh
bowlers.description: Medium-fast all-rounder (bowler card, paired with the new Elite batter card). Useful 6th bowler at international level when fit. Hard back-of-length pacer with no specific skill at the Bronze tier.

bowlers.name: Jofra Archer
bowlers.description: Express pace at 145+ kph. Yorker maestro with late seam off the deck and world-class slower-ball variations (slower bouncer, knuckle ball). When fit, unplayable. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Stuart Broad
bowlers.description: Legend — retired 2023, all-time-great new-ball seamer with 56 T20I wickets. Late outswing from the natural angle, hits the deck hard with bounce. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Adil Rashid
bowlers.description: England's #1 leg-spinner. T20 WC 2022 winner, middle-overs wicket-taker. Stock leg-break drifting onto the pads with a disguised googly that breaks back into the stumps.

bowlers.name: Mark Wood
bowlers.description: Express pacer (150+ kph when fit). Hits the deck hard with bounce, late seam off the surface. Death-overs short ball specialist.

bowlers.name: Tymal Mills
bowlers.description: LH death-overs specialist. Knuckle ball + slower-ball cutter variations are his trade. RCB IPL star, current T20I returnee when fit. Skill: **Slower**.

bowlers.name: Sam Curran
bowlers.description: Left-arm medium-pacer (bowler card, paired with his Silver batter card via the all-rounder split). T20 WC 2022 Player of the Tournament. Death-overs specialist with no specific skill at the Silver tier — just clever yorker-length lines.

bowlers.name: Reece Topley
bowlers.description: Tall left-arm seamer. Powerplay specialist on the corridor with the new ball. Faces the Silver tier-based wide-call risk on Outside off (25%). Injury-prone.

bowlers.name: Jamie Overton
bowlers.description: Right-arm pacer. Hit-the-deck back-of-length attack with bounce. BBL-style power-bowler with limited T20I exposure so far.

bowlers.name: Liam Livingstone
bowlers.description: Part-time leg-spin. Useful overs filler with no specific skill at the Bronze tier.

bowlers.name: Will Jacks
bowlers.description: Part-time off-spin. Handy if conditions suit.

bowlers.name: Mason Crane
bowlers.description: Leg-spinner. County specialist, rarely selected internationally.

bowlers.name: Saqib Mahmood
bowlers.description: Fast-medium. Good length off stump with some seam.

bowlers.name: Tom Hartley
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox. Emerging spinner, minimal T20I experience.

bowlers.name: Kagiso Rabada
bowlers.description: Best SA T20 quick of his era. Yorker maestro, late seam off the deck, signature slow-ball cutter. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Imran Tahir
bowlers.description: Legend leg-spinner — retired internationals 2019, 38 T20Is, IPL/CPL/PSL star, world's #1 ranked T20I bowler at his peak. Disguised wrong'un + drift away from RH bats. Wild celebrations after every wicket. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Anrich Nortje
bowlers.description: 150kph+ raw pace. Hits the deck hard with bounce; back-of-length intimidator.

bowlers.name: Tabraiz Shamsi
bowlers.description: Left-arm wrist spinner. Mystery deliveries with a disguised wrong'un breaking back into RH bats.

bowlers.name: Dale Steyn
bowlers.description: Legend — retired 2021, all-time-great fast bowler, 47 T20Is. Late outswing, hits the deck hard with bounce, fierce competitor. Skill: **Seam**.

bowlers.name: Marco Jansen
bowlers.description: Tall left-arm quick. Full Outside off with new-ball movement and steep bounce. Faces the Silver tier wide-call risk on Outside off (25%).

bowlers.name: Keshav Maharaj
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox. Economical middle-overs operator with subtle drift away from RH bats.

bowlers.name: Lungi Ngidi
bowlers.description: Tall right-arm seamer. Good-length new-ball threat with bounce. No specific skill at the Silver tier.

bowlers.name: Ottneil Baartman
bowlers.description: Emerging right-arm fast bowler. Domestic standout.

bowlers.name: Aiden Markram
bowlers.description: Part-time off-spin (all-rounder split — Silver batter card above). Handy filler overs.

bowlers.name: Gerald Coetzee
bowlers.description: Young right-arm fast bowler. Raw pace, erratic radar.

bowlers.name: Wiaan Mulder
bowlers.description: Medium-fast all-rounder (bowler split — Bronze batter above). Fills overs.

bowlers.name: Andile Phehlukwayo
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast. SA's go-to white-ball all-rounder 2017–2022 (50+ T20I caps). Skiddy slingshot action; lower-order LH hitter in real life but bowler-only card here.

bowlers.name: Trent Boult
bowlers.description: Best NZ T20 quick of his era. Late left-arm swing with the new ball, signature slower-bouncer at the death. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Daniel Vettori
bowlers.description: Legend — retired 2015, LA orthodox great, 102 T20Is, IPL stalwart. Subtle drift, disguised topspinner that skids on, miserly economy in middle overs. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Tim Southee
bowlers.description: NZ swing legend, second-most T20I wickets in history. Outswing on the corridor with the new ball. Faces Gold tier wide-call risk on Outside off (15%).

bowlers.name: Ish Sodhi
bowlers.description: Right-arm leg-spinner. Disguised wrong'un breaking back into RH bats; consistent middle-overs threat.

bowlers.name: Mitchell McClenaghan
bowlers.description: Retired 2020, LH death-overs specialist, MI IPL star. Slingy left-arm angle with cutters and slower-ball variations at the death. Skill: **Slower**.

bowlers.name: Lockie Ferguson
bowlers.description: Express right-arm pace (150kph+). Short ball is his weapon. No specific skill at the Silver tier.

bowlers.name: Mitchell Santner
bowlers.description: NZ T20I captain. Left-arm orthodox, IPL 2024 CSK star. Economical middle-overs operator.

bowlers.name: Matt Henry
bowlers.description: Right-arm swing bowler, powerplay specialist. Faces Silver tier wide-call risk on Outside off (25%).

bowlers.name: Glenn Phillips
bowlers.description: Part-time right-arm off-spin (all-rounder split — Gold batter card above). Handy filler overs.

bowlers.name: Michael Bracewell
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spin all-rounder (bowler split — Bronze batter above). Domestic-tier overs filler.

bowlers.name: Ben Sears
bowlers.description: Young right-arm fast bowler. Good length with pace, raw at international level.

bowlers.name: Rachin Ravindra
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox spin part-time (all-rounder split — Silver batter card above).

bowlers.name: Cole McConchie
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spin domestic specialist.

bowlers.name: Shaheen Shah Afridi
bowlers.description: Best PAK T20 quick of his era. Left-arm late inswing with the new ball, signature slower-bouncer at the death. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Saeed Ajmal
bowlers.description: Legend off-spinner — retired 2017, 64 T20Is, world's best spinner at his peak. The doosra (carrom) and disguised drift made him almost unplayable for a stretch. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Haris Rauf
bowlers.description: Express right-arm pace, death-overs specialist (150kph+). Slow-ball cutter is his signature variation.

bowlers.name: Naseem Shah
bowlers.description: Young express right-arm quick. Hits the deck hard with bounce, new-ball seam off the surface.

bowlers.name: Mohammad Amir
bowlers.description: Retired 2020, LH swing legend, 50 T20Is. T20 WC 2009 / Champions Trophy 2017 hero with the new ball. Late inswing back into the right-hander from a natural left-arm angle. Skill: **Swing**.

bowlers.name: Mohammad Nawaz
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox all-rounder. Economical middle-overs operator.

bowlers.name: Abrar Ahmed
bowlers.description: Mystery right-arm spinner. Carrom ball and disguised wrong'un — but no specific skill chip at the Silver tier per v1.1.

bowlers.name: Usman Tariq
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spinner. Promising emerging talent attacking the pads to RH bats.

bowlers.name: Salman Mirza
bowlers.description: Left-arm quick, emerging PSL/IPL prospect (LSG 2025).

bowlers.name: Faheem Ashraf
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast all-rounder (bowler split — Bronze batter above).

bowlers.name: Mohammad Hasnain
bowlers.description: Right-arm quick. Pace without control; high-variance Bronze profile.

bowlers.name: Iftikhar Ahmed
bowlers.description: Part-time right-arm off-spin (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Emergency filler.

bowlers.name: Shadab Khan
bowlers.description: Right-arm leg-spinner (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Was PAK's Gold-tier leggie at his peak; current form has dropped him to Bronze (loses the googly skill chip).

bowlers.name: Wanindu Hasaranga
bowlers.description: SL T20 leg-spinner extraordinaire. Multi-time T20 WC top wicket-taker. Disguised wrong'un + skiddy topspinner. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Lasith Malinga
bowlers.description: Legend — retired 2021, 84 T20Is, all-time-great T20 death bowler, MI IPL talisman. Slingy round-arm action delivers the toe-crushing yorker; the slow-ball cutter is just as iconic. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Matheesha Pathirana
bowlers.description: Slingy right-arm fast bowler. Malinga-style action with death-overs slow-ball cutter signature.

bowlers.name: Maheesh Theekshana
bowlers.description: Mystery right-arm off-spinner. Carrom ball specialist, disguised release.

bowlers.name: Ajantha Mendis
bowlers.description: Mystery spin pioneer — retired 2015, 39 T20Is. The original carrom-ball specialist who shocked the cricketing world before everyone learned to read him. Skill: **Carrom**.

bowlers.name: Dushmantha Chameera
bowlers.description: Right-arm quick. Full and fast through the stumps.

bowlers.name: Dilshan Madushanka
bowlers.description: Left-arm seamer. Swing with the new ball. Faces Silver tier wide-call risk on Outside off (25%).

bowlers.name: Dunith Wellalage
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox all-rounder (bowler split — Bronze batter above). Economical middle-overs operator.

bowlers.name: Eshan Malinga
bowlers.description: Son of Lasith. Slingy emerging right-arm quick.

bowlers.name: Kamindu Mendis
bowlers.description: Ambidextrous part-time spinner (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). Switches off-spin/leg-spin between deliveries — but no skill chip at the Bronze tier.

bowlers.name: Dasun Shanaka
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium pace (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Fills overs at the death.

bowlers.name: Nuwan Thushara
bowlers.description: Right-arm slingy quick. T20 WC 2024 hat-trick taker vs Bangladesh. Yorker-length attacking the stumps.

bowlers.name: Angelo Mathews
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-pace (bowler-only card). SL all-format icon, 200+ international caps. Bowled key T20I overs in his prime — gentle pace into the corridor.

bowlers.name: Sunil Narine
bowlers.description: Retired from WI internationals (last 2019) but the most feared T20 league mystery spinner globally. Right-arm off-spin with the carrom ball; skiddy quicker one as the disguised follow-up. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Dwayne Bravo
bowlers.description: Retired 2021, 91 T20Is, **all-time-best T20 death-overs cutter king**, CSK IPL legend. The slow-ball cutter and the disguised slower bouncer redefined T20 death bowling. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Akeal Hosein
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox. Powerplay specialist with subtle drift away from RH bats.

bowlers.name: Alzarri Joseph
bowlers.description: Right-arm express pace. Current WI T20I regular, hits the deck hard with bounce and seam off the surface.

bowlers.name: Carlos Brathwaite
bowlers.description: T20 WC 2016 final hero — **4 sixes in 4 balls** off Stokes to win the trophy. 41 T20Is, RH all-rounder. Death-overs cutter and slower-ball variations. Skill: **Slower**.

bowlers.name: Andre Russell
bowlers.description: Right-arm express (bowler card; Gold batter split above). Death-overs cutters and yorkers were Elite-tier at his peak; current load-managed role drops him to Silver and strips the skill chip.

bowlers.name: Shamar Joseph
bowlers.description: Right-arm raw express pace (150kph+). Recent Test breakout star, growing T20I role.

bowlers.name: Jason Holder
bowlers.description: Tall right-arm seamer, all-format WI icon and former captain. Hits a heavy length with bounce; T20 form has waned, hence Silver not Gold. Faces Silver tier wide-call risk on Outside off (25%).

bowlers.name: Jayden Seales
bowlers.description: Young right-arm quick. Good length with pace.

bowlers.name: Roston Chase
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spin (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Fills middle overs.

bowlers.name: Matthew Forde
bowlers.description: Young right-arm seamer. Developing.

bowlers.name: Gudakesh Motie
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox. Current WI T20I middle-overs operator — no skill chip at Bronze per v1.1.

bowlers.name: Romario Shepherd
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast all-rounder (bowler split — Bronze batter above). Fills overs at the death.

bowlers.name: Mustafizur Rahman
bowlers.description: Left-arm off-cutter specialist. IPL star with multiple Purple Cap-tier seasons. Death-overs cutter + signature slower bouncer. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Mashrafe Mortaza
bowlers.description: Legend — retired 2020, 54 T20Is, beloved BAN captain figure across two decades. Right-arm new-ball specialist with smart slower-ball variations at the death. The heart and soul of Bangladesh cricket. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Taskin Ahmed
bowlers.description: Best current BAN quick. Right-arm pace with bounce, hits the deck hard with seam off the stumps.

bowlers.name: Shakib Al Hasan
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox legend (bowler card; Gold batter split above). Subtle drift away from RH bats with metronomic accuracy.

bowlers.name: Abdur Razzak
bowlers.description: Legend left-arm orthodox — retired 2018, 34 T20Is. Stock arm-ball drifting away from RH bats with smart loop. BAN's most-prolific spinner across formats for a decade. Skill: **Drift**.

bowlers.name: Mehidy Hasan Miraz
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spinner (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Middle-overs control bowler.

bowlers.name: Nahid Rana
bowlers.description: Right-arm raw express pace (150kph+). Exciting young quick attacking the stumps.

bowlers.name: Rishad Hossain
bowlers.description: Right-arm leg-spinner (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Gets turn and bounce — but no skill chip at the Silver tier.

bowlers.name: Nasum Ahmed
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox. Economical middle-overs filler.

bowlers.name: Shoriful Islam
bowlers.description: Left-arm quick. Developing new-ball threat.

bowlers.name: Hasan Mahmud
bowlers.description: Young right-arm seamer.

bowlers.name: Tanzim Hasan Sakib
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast. Rising new-ball option.

bowlers.name: Soumya Sarkar
bowlers.description: Part-time right-arm medium (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). Emergency overs.

bowlers.name: Blessing Muzarabani
bowlers.description: Best ZIM quick in years. Tall right-arm express pace with steep bounce; signature slow-ball bouncer at the death. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Heath Streak
bowlers.description: Honorary Elite — ZIM's all-time greatest bowler, retired 2005 at the dawn of T20Is. Right-arm fast-medium with late seam, slower-ball variations. Captain figure, ZIM's all-format spearhead through their golden generation. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Sikandar Raza
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spin (all-rounder split — Elite batter above). Skiddy quicker arm-ball is his middle-overs wicket-taker.

bowlers.name: Richard Ngarava
bowlers.description: Left-arm quick. New-ball inswing specialist. Faces Gold tier wide-call risk on Outside off (15%).

bowlers.name: Ray Price
bowlers.description: LA orthodox legend — retired 2014, 21 T20Is. Subtle drift away from RH bats with miserly economy. ZIM's longtime spin spearhead through the 2000s/early 2010s. Skill: **Drift**.

bowlers.name: Ryan Burl
bowlers.description: Right-arm leg-spinner (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). Gets turn and bounce — but no skill chip at Silver.

bowlers.name: Luke Jongwe
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast all-rounder. Death-overs cutter style. Bowler-only card (his bat is tail-ender quality).

bowlers.name: Sean Williams
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox (all-rounder split — Gold batter above). Economical middle-overs operator.

bowlers.name: Tendai Chatara
bowlers.description: Experienced right-arm seamer. Good length with wobble.

bowlers.name: Wessly Madhevere
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium pace (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). Part-time filler.

bowlers.name: Wellington Masakadza
bowlers.description: Right-arm quick. Pace and bounce.

bowlers.name: Milton Shumba
bowlers.description: Part-time right-arm medium (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Emergency filler.

bowlers.name: Brian Bennett
bowlers.description: Young right-arm seamer, developing.

bowlers.name: Rashid Khan
bowlers.description: Greatest T20 bowler alive. Right-arm leg-spin with a lethal disguised googly and skiddy quicker one. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Dawlat Zadran
bowlers.description: Retired 2021, RH express pace, 46 T20Is. Hit-the-deck new-ball specialist, slower-ball variations at the death. AFG's first-generation pace spearhead. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Fazalhaq Farooqi
bowlers.description: Left-arm quick. T20 WC 2024 leading wicket-taker (17). Powerplay inswing specialist. Faces Gold tier wide-call risk on Outside off (15%).

bowlers.name: Mujeeb Ur Rahman
bowlers.description: Right-handed mystery off-spinner with the carrom ball. IPL star, was world's youngest international debutant.

bowlers.name: Naveen-ul-Haq
bowlers.description: Right-arm slingy seamer. KKR IPL pick, death-overs operator.

bowlers.name: Noor Ahmad
bowlers.description: Young left-arm wrist spinner. KKR IPL star — loses the skill chip at Silver per v1.1.

bowlers.name: Azmatullah Omarzai
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast all-rounder (bowler split — Silver batter above). Hits the deck with seam, but no skill chip at Silver.

bowlers.name: Mohammad Nabi
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spin veteran (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). Economical, wise.

bowlers.name: Gulbadin Naib
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium pace (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Fills overs.

bowlers.name: AM Ghazanfar
bowlers.description: Young right-arm off-spinner.

bowlers.name: Karim Janat
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Death-overs cutter style.

bowlers.name: Yamin Ahmadzai
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast.

bowlers.name: Josh Little
bowlers.description: Ireland's best-ever fast bowler. Left-arm express with new-ball inswing + signature slow-ball cutter. IPL/T20 league regular (GT, MI). Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Boyd Rankin
bowlers.description: Multi-nation career — IRE → ENG → IRE. RH tall pace, retired 2022, 56 T20Is across both countries. Bounce off the deck, late seam, slower-ball variations at the death. Two-skill Elite per the v1.1 rule.

bowlers.name: Mark Adair
bowlers.description: Right-arm fast-medium. Death-overs specialist with seam off the deck.

bowlers.name: Curtis Campher
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). T20 WC 2021 4-in-4 hero with new-ball outswing. Faces Gold tier wide-call risk on Outside off (15%).

bowlers.name: Tim Murtagh
bowlers.description: Retired 2024 — RH swing legend. The disciplined county-cricket craftsman. Late outswing on a perfect line, miserly economy. Iconic Test moments (5/13 vs ENG at Lord's 2019). Skill: **Swing**.

bowlers.name: George Dockrell
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox (all-rounder split — Silver batter above). Experienced, economical middle-overs operator.

bowlers.name: Gareth Delany
bowlers.description: Right-arm off-spin (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above). Middle-overs filler.

bowlers.name: Barry McCarthy
bowlers.description: Right-arm fast-medium. Hits the pads with pace.

bowlers.name: Graham Hume
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast (all-rounder split — Bronze batter above).

bowlers.name: Craig Young
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast. Steady but unspectacular.

bowlers.name: Ben White
bowlers.description: Young right-arm leg-spinner. Developing.

bowlers.name: Fionn Hand
bowlers.description: Young right-arm quick. Raw pace.

bowlers.name: Harry Tector
bowlers.description: Part-time right-arm off-spin (all-rounder split — Gold batter above). Emergency filler.

bowlers.name: Sandeep Lamichhane
bowlers.description: Right-arm leg-spinner. 95+ T20Is, IPL star (DC), Nepal's most exported cricketer. Disguised wrong'un — but loses the skill chip at Silver per v1.1.

bowlers.name: Ehsan Khan
bowlers.description: Current RH off-spinner. Hong Kong's middle-overs operator. Tight lines, good drift.

bowlers.name: Mark Watt
bowlers.description: Left-arm orthodox spinner. Instantly recognizable on the field (sunglasses, slow walk-up). T20 WC squad regular.

bowlers.name: Saurabh Netravalkar
bowlers.description: Left-arm quick. T20 WC 2024 Super Over hero vs Pakistan — bowled the over that won USA the match.

bowlers.name: Roelof van der Merwe
bowlers.description: Multi-nation: SA → NED. LA wrist-spin all-rounder, IPL/county veteran. Disguised wrong'un, smart middle-overs operator. Skill: **Googly**.

bowlers.name: Paul van Meekeren
bowlers.description: Right-arm fast-medium. T20 WC 2022 famous 4/11 spell vs South Africa.

bowlers.name: Logan van Beek
bowlers.description: Current RA pace all-rounder, 30+ T20Is, T20 WC 2024 squad.

bowlers.name: David Wiese
bowlers.description: Right-arm medium-fast all-rounder, ex-South Africa. NAM's go-to death-overs operator at T20 WC 2021/2024. Bowler-only card.

bowlers.name: JJ Smit
bowlers.description: Current LH pace all-rounder. T20 WC 2021/2024 squads. Hit-the-deck back-of-length style.

situations.name: DRS Review
situations.flavor: Not out! The batsman survives on review.
situations.description: If your batsman gets out this ball, the dismissal is overturned and it becomes a **dot ball** instead. Does not change any other outcome — only triggers on a wicket result.

situations.name: Power Surge
situations.flavor: The field is up! Time to attack.
situations.description:
All outcomes on your batsman's card **upgrade one tier** this ball:
- Dot → 1
- 1 → 2
- 2 → 4
- 4 → 6

**Weaknesses still trigger as wickets** — this does not protect against getting out. Only improves scoring zones.

situations.name: Retired Out
situations.flavor: New batsman walks to the crease mid-over.
situations.description:
Discard your played batsman card **before** the delivery resolves. Immediately play a replacement batsman from your hand. The new batsman faces the delivery instead.

If you have no other batsman in hand, this card cannot be played.

situations.name: Switch Hit
situations.flavor: The batsman switches stance! Off side becomes leg side.
situations.description:
Your batsman's card zones are **mirrored** for this ball:
- Off stump ↔ Leg stump
- 5th stump ↔ (treated as Leg stump)
- Middle stump stays Middle stump
- Wide outside off ↔ (treated as Leg stump)

The bowler's delivery zone does NOT change — only which part of the batsman's card it maps to. Fielding positions are NOT mirrored.

situations.name: Trot Down
situations.flavor: The batsman charges down the pitch!
situations.description:
Your batsman pre-meditates walking down the track, changing the effective length of the delivery:
- **Good length → Full** (the batsman has closed the distance)
- **Short → Good length** (the short ball arrives at good length height)
- **Full → Full** (stays the same, already full)

The bowler's line stays unchanged. Only the length shifts.

situations.name: No Ball
situations.flavor: Foot fault! The umpire's called it!
situations.description:
The bowler oversteps the crease. This delivery:
- **Cancels any wicket** — if the result was a wicket, it becomes a dot ball.
- **+1 run** to the batting team (the no-ball extra).
- **Re-bowled** — this delivery doesn't count against the innings ball total.

The runs from the actual outcome (e.g. a 4 or 6 if the batter still hit it) ALSO stand and are added on top of the +1 extra.

> **Important — no free hit.** Unlike real T20 cricket, the No Ball card does **not** grant the next delivery free-hit status. The wicket protection applies only to *this* ball; the re-bowled ball resolves under normal rules.

situations.name: Shuffle Across
situations.flavor: The batsman shuffles across his stumps.
situations.description:
Your batsman steps across to the off side, treating the bowler's line as one stump further toward leg on their card:
- **Outside off → Off stump**
- **Off stump → Middle stump**
- **Middle stump → Leg stump**
- **Leg stump → Leg stump** (clamped — can't shuffle further)

Length is unchanged. Inverse of Day 5 Pitch.

**Wide-call risk.** If the bowler delivered on **Leg stump** when Shuffle Across is played, the batter has shuffled past the line — the umpire calls a wide. +1 extra run, ball is re-bowled.

situations.name: Deep in the Crease
situations.flavor: The batsman steps back, deep in his crease.
situations.description:
Your batsman creates extra time by stepping back. The effective length shifts outward (toward the bowler):
- **Good length → Short** (good length is met higher and earlier)
- **Full → Good length** (full balls become drivable good-length deliveries)
- **Short → wide call** (short balls bounce too high — auto-wide)

The bowler's line stays unchanged. Inverse of Trot Down.

situations.name: Old School Cricket Only
situations.flavor: Play the game properly! No tricks today.
situations.description:
**Cancels the opponent's situation card entirely.** Both situation cards are discarded and the ball resolves with just the batsman vs bowler cards — no situation effects.

If the opponent didn't play a situation card, this is wasted.

situations.name: Mankad
situations.flavor: The bowler spots the batsman backing up too far...
situations.description:
Your opponent must **discard their played batsman** and play a different batsman from their hand. The new batsman faces the delivery instead.

If the opponent has no other batsman in hand, the original batsman stays but all their outcomes **downgrade one tier** this ball (treated as facing an extra adjective).

situations.name: Review Appeal
situations.flavor: Howzaaaaat! The bowler appeals for everything.
situations.description:
If this ball results in a **dot ball**, it becomes a wicket appeal — **40% chance** the dot is upgraded to a wicket (LBW/caught behind).

Does not trigger on any other outcome (runs scored or existing wicket). Only dots become potential wickets.

situations.name: Cramps
situations.flavor: The bowler pulls up mid run-up. Replacement needed.
situations.description:
Discard your played bowler card **before** the delivery resolves. Immediately play a replacement bowler from your hand. The new bowler delivers instead.

If you have no other bowler in hand, this card cannot be played.

situations.name: Invariable Bounce
situations.flavor: The ball doesn't come onto the bat. Everything is a mis-hit.
situations.description:
All outcomes on the opponent's batsman card **downgrade one tier** this ball:
- 6 → 4
- 4 → 2
- 2 → 1
- 1 → Dot
- Dot → Dot (stays)
- Weakness → Weakness (stays)

This stacks with the bowler's adjective downgrade if applicable. A batsman facing an adjective they're not resistant to PLUS Invariable Bounce gets **double downgraded**.

situations.name: Day 5 Pitch
situations.flavor: The pitch is crumbling. The ball is doing things.
situations.description:
Your bowler's delivery line shifts **one line away from the batsman's body**:
- Leg stump → Middle stump
- Middle stump → Off stump
- Off stump → 5th stump
- 5th stump → Wide outside off
- Wide outside off → Wide outside off (stays)

The length stays unchanged. The bowler's adjective and fielding still apply normally.

situations.name: Third Umpire Distracted by Biryani
situations.flavor: The third umpire's lunch arrived. Decisions get… liberal.
situations.description:
A counter to extras. When played by the bowling side:
- Any **No Ball** the batting side played has its effects **fully cancelled** — no extra run, no re-bowl, no wicket overturn.
- Any **wide call** that would have happened (tier-based wide on Outside off, leg-side wide from Shuffle Across, Outside-off wide from Day 5 Pitch, Short auto-wide from Deep in the Crease) is converted to a **regular dot ball** — no extra run, ball counts.

If neither a No Ball was played nor a wide would have been called, this card is wasted.

situations.name: Old School Cricket Only
situations.flavor: Play the game properly! No tricks today.
situations.description:
**Cancels the opponent's situation card entirely.** Both situation cards are discarded and the ball resolves with just the batsman vs bowler cards — no situation effects.

If the opponent didn't play a situation card, this is wasted.
